signal_title: Amendment to the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act
risk_rationale: The draft bill proposes amendments to the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act, which could introduce new compliance requirements for online platforms like TikTok. This poses a material compliance gap that may require immediate product changes to align with the new legal standards.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potentially stricter age verification and parental consent mechanisms for minors under 13.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The amendment may require enhanced age verification and parental consent mechanisms, which are not currently specified in the baseline.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 13-15 years is private by default with no opt-out allowed.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potentially stricter privacy controls for minors under 16.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The amendment may necessitate stricter privacy controls for minors, impacting current settings for 13-15 year olds.

remediations.title: Enhance Age Verification and Parental Consent Mechanisms
remediations.description: Develop and implement enhanced age verification processes and parental consent mechanisms to comply with the updated COPPA requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification process updated to meet new legal standards
Parental consent mechanisms implemented for users under 13

remediations.title: Strengthen Privacy Controls for Minors
remediations.description: Review and update privacy settings for minors to ensure compliance with potentially stricter privacy requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy settings for minors reviewed and updated
Compliance with new privacy standards verified

business_impact: Potential increase in compliance costs and engineering resources to implement new age verification and privacy controls. May affect user onboarding processes and user experience for minors.
recommended_actions: Monitor the progress of the draft bill and prepare for potential changes.
Initiate a cross-functional task force to assess and implement necessary changes.
Engage with policymakers to understand the implications and provide feedback on the draft bill.
